mfdb 7.4-0-999
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Import hydrographic data into MFDB

    Import hydrographic data into MFDB

    import_hydrography_cmems.Rmd
    library(mfdb)

    Hydrographic products are available from numerous sources. Here, we provide a working example where outputs from a hydrographic model are:

    • downloaded from Copernicus Marine Service https://marine.copernicus.eu/
    • aggregated over ICES rectangles
    • imported into MFDB

    Download monthly hydrographic data

    CMEMS Baltic Sea Physical Reanalysis product provides a physical reanalysis for the whole Baltic Sea area from January 1993 and up to minus 1-1.5 year compared to real time. The product is produced by using the ice-ocean model NEMO-Nordic (based on NEMO-3.6, Nucleus for European Modelling of the Ocean) together with a LSEIK data assimilation scheme, https://data.marine.copernicus.eu/?option=com_csw&view=details&product_id=BALTICSEA_REANALYSIS_PHY_003_011 As a demonstration we retrieve one year of already processed monthly means of bottom temperature (bottomT), but hydrographic products are also available for separate depth layers and at higher spatiotemporal resolution, and can be aggregated before import according to the needs. Note that an account has to be created to download data from Copernicus Marine Service using the script below.

    stopifnot(requireNamespace("CFtime", quietly = TRUE)) # Needed to parse dates

options(
  CopernicusMarine_uid = "...",
  CopernicusMarine_pwd = "..."
)

y <- 2018 # year of interest
dat <- CopernicusMarine::cms_download_subset(
  product       = "BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003_011",
  layer         = "cmems_mod_bal_phy_my_P1M-m",
  variable      = "bottomT",
  region        = c(17, 54, 30, 58.5),     # c(lon_min, lat_min, lon_max, lat_max)
  timerange     = paste0(y, c("-01-01", "-12-31")),
  verticalrange = c(0, 1)
)

    Download ICES rectangles 

    temp <- tempfile()
download.file(url="https://gis.ices.dk/shapefiles/ICES_StatRec_mapto_ICES_Areas.zip", temp)
unzip(temp, exdir="data_provided/ices_rect")
unlink(temp)

    Aggregate hydrographic data by ICES rectangles 

    # load ICES rect
ices.rect <- sf::st_read("data_provided/ices_rect/StatRec_map_Areas_Full_20170124.dbf", quiet = T)

# Aggregate bottomT by ICES rect
ar <- aggregate(dat, ices.rect$geometry, FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE) |> dplyr::pull(bottomT)
dimnames(ar) <- list(ICESNAME = ices.rect$ICESNAME, month = 1:12)
hydroVar <-
  as.data.frame.table(ar, responseName = "sob") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(sob)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    year = y,
    month = as.numeric(month)
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(
    rect = ICESNAME,
  )

write.table(hydroVar, file = 'hydroVar.txt')

    Combine ICES rectangles with rnaturalearth::ne_countries() to get sea surface area 

    ices.rect <- sf::st_read("data_provided/ices_rect/StatRec_map_Areas_Full_20170124.dbf", quiet = T)

ices.rect.sea <-
  ices.rect |>
  dplyr::filter(ICESNAME %in% hydroVar$rect) |>
  sf::st_difference(
    rnaturalearth::ne_countries(returnclass = "sf", continent="Europe", scale="large") |>
    sf::st_union()
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    areaSea_km2 = sf::st_area(geometry) |> units::set_units(km^2)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    rect = ICESNAME,
    areaSea_km2 = areaSea_km2
  ) |>
  sf::st_drop_geometry()

write.table(sf::st_drop_geometry(ices.rect.sea), file = 'ices.rect.sea.txt')

    Import bottom temperature into mfdb

    Import ICES rectangles and import the hydrographic data as a survey index. Then we can retrieve the hydrographic data as a weighted mean using ICES rectangle as a weighting variable:

    mdb <- mfdb(tempfile(fileext = '.duckdb'))

ices.rect.sea <- read.table('ices.rect.sea.txt')
hydroVar <- read.table('hydroVar.txt') |>
  dplyr::inner_join(ices.rect.sea, by = "rect")

# import ICES rectangles
mfdb_import_area(mdb, data.frame(
    name = as.character(ices.rect.sea$rect),
    size = ices.rect.sea$areaSea_km2))

# import area size as index for later use
mfdb_import_cs_taxonomy(mdb, "index_type", data.frame(name=c("ices_rect", "bottom_temp")))
mfdb_import_survey_index(mdb,
                         data_source="area_ices_rect",
                         data.frame(index_type = "ices_rect",
                                        year       = hydroVar$year,
                                        month      = hydroVar$month,
                                        areacell   = as.character(hydroVar$rect),
                                        value      = as.numeric(hydroVar$areaSea_km2)))

# import avg bottom temperature by month and ICES rect (under the 'length' field)
mfdb_import_survey_index(mdb,
                   data_source="bottom_temp_CMEMS",
                   data.frame(year       = hydroVar$year,
                              month      = hydroVar$month,
                              areacell   = as.character(hydroVar$rect),
                              value     = hydroVar$sob,
                              index_type = "bottom_temp",
                              stringsAsFactors = TRUE))

# extract mean bottom temperature by quarter and area (weighted mean using rectangle area)
dat <- mfdb_survey_index_mean(mdb, c(), list(
                                       ## area = mfdb_unaggregated(),
                                       area = mfdb_group('a1'=c('42G7','42G8','42G9','43G7','43G8','43G9'),
                                                         'a2'=c('44G7','44G8','44G9','45G7','45G8','45G9')),
                                       timestep = mfdb_timestep_quarterly,
                                       year = 2018,
                                       index_type = "bottom_temp",
                                       data_source = 'bottom_temp_CMEMS'),
                              scale_index = 'area_size')[[1]]
dat <- dat[,c('year', 'step', 'area', 'mean')]
dat
    ##   year step area     mean
## 1 2018    1   a1 5.065878
## 2 2018    1   a2 5.277945
## 3 2018    2   a1 5.535443
## 4 2018    2   a2 5.409325
## 5 2018    3   a1 6.778031
## 6 2018    3   a2 6.149436
## 7 2018    4   a1 6.090254
## 8 2018    4   a2 5.768832
    mfdb_disconnect(mdb)
options(unittest.output = NULL)

    Developed by Jamie Lentin, Bjarki Thor Elvarsson.
